company.name: Biohaven Ltd.
company.subtitle: Pre-pivotal multi-platform — Kv7 + MoDE/TRAP degraders + obesity

market.notes: Selective Kv7.2/7.3 activator. 40x therapeutic index advantage. OLE data: majority ≥50% seizure reduction at 6 months. RISE 2/3 topline H2 2026. Differentiated safety (low CNS AEs vs other ASMs).

us.patientsK.note: NIH/CDC: ~1.2M US treated focal (partial-onset) epilepsy; ~60% of ~3.4M total epilepsy prevalence.

eu.patientsK.note: ILAE EU: ~2.5M treated focal epilepsy EU27+UK; ~6M total epilepsy prevalence.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~30M global epilepsy; ~18M focal; 80% treatment gap in LMICs.

us.wtpPct.note: ~85% - Medicare Part D and commercial cover Vimpat/Briviact/Xcopri; 30% refractory drive branded AED use.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~70% - EMA approved all modern AEDs; generics dominate first-line, branded for refractory.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% - Japan broad coverage; emerging markets heavy generic (levetiracetam/carbamazepine).

us.priceK.note: Blended $4K: levetiracetam generic $0.5K (70% of pts), Xcopri $12K, Briviact $14K, Vimpat generic $2K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$2K blended; EU net ~50% of US, generic levetiracetam backbone.

penPct.note: ~70% peak - large treated pool but branded share limited by generic levetiracetam first-line.

us.reachPct.note: Ph2/3 novel Kv7 mechanism in 1.2M US epilepsy patients; 5% reach reflects 3rd+ line ASM positioning and Ph2/3 stage

us.priceK.note: Specialty oral epilepsy ASM class ~$15K/yr (cenobamate, brivaracetam benchmarks)

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 40% -- estimated launch WTP pending Phase 3 + label

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 3% of 2500K; EMA pathway at phase3

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 30% -- EMA path pending Phase 3; HTA gating expected

eu.priceK.note: EU net $8K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 1% of 18000K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at phase3

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 8% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $3K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: First-in-class extracellular IgG degrader. First patient: complete TSI suppression + thyroid normalization within weeks. >87% IgG reduction in healthy volunteers. Pivotal planned H2 2026.

us.patientsK.note: ATA 2024 + Amgen Tepezza context: ~1.2M US Graves disease; ~100K Thyroid Eye Disease (TED) moderate-severe subset (Tepezza-addressable).

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet/EUGOGO: ~1M EU Graves; ~80K TED moderate-severe eligible for teprotumumab.

row.patientsK.note: Global Graves ~50M prevalence; treatable pool ROW ~150K TED.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- Medicare/commercial cover Tepezza (teprotumumab) for TED post OPTIC Ph3; methimazole/PTU generic for Graves hyperthyroidism.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- EMA approved Tepezza 2025; HTA negotiation G-BA/NICE ongoing.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Japan PMDA Tepezza review; emerging methimazole generic-dominant.

us.priceK.note: $350K/course anchored to Amgen/Horizon Tepezza (teprotumumab) 8-infusion course WAC 2024; methimazole generic ($200).

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$200K/course Tepezza post HTA; methimazole near-zero.

penPct.note: ~15% peak -- TED moderate-severe captive subset; Viridian VRDN-001/003 Ph3 competition 2025+.

us.reachPct.note: Ph1b first-in-class MoDE IgG degrader; 4% reach in 500K Graves' reflects early stage + need to displace antithyroid drugs

us.priceK.note: Specialty biologic for autoimmune endocrine ~$30K/yr; premium to generic antithyroid drugs but below mAb pricing

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 30% -- speculative -- Phase 1 proof-of-concept only

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 2% of 80K; EMA pathway at phase1

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 22% -- EU access TBD; EMA pathway unclear at stage

eu.priceK.note: EU net $15K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 0.5% of 150K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at phase1

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 8% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $5K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: Selective Gd-IgA1 degrader — targets pathogenic driver without broad immunosuppression. >80% Gd-IgA1 reduction. Early IgAN patient data: biomarker + clinical responses (proteinuria, hematuria improvement). Pivotal initiating Q1 2026.

us.patientsK.note: NKF: ~130K US IgAN prevalent; ~40% progress to ESRD within 20yr; most common primary GN globally.

eu.patientsK.note: ERA registry: ~150K EU27+UK IgAN; Italy/France highest biopsy rates; MEST-C scoring standard.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~1.5M (Asia Pacific 40-50% of primary GN biopsies); Japan screening program drives detection.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Tarpeyo (budesonide) + Filspari covered post-2023 FDA approvals; Medicare + commercial strong.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- EMA approved Kinpeygo/Filspari; NICE positive; RAASi + SGLT2i baseline therapy.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan JSN guidelines tonsillectomy + steroids; China TCM + supportive care common.

us.priceK.note: Blended $65K: Tarpeyo $15K/9mo course (30%) + Filspari $120K (20%) + RAASi/SGLT2i generic $2K baseline.

eu.priceK.note: ~$32K blended -- Kinpeygo ~$25K course + Filspari ~$70K net + generic backbone.

penPct.note: ~30% peak -- novel agents stacking (Tarpeyo + Filspari + SGLT2i); sibeprenlimab/atrasentan pipeline.

us.reachPct.note: Ph1 expansion TRAP degrader; 5% reach in 130K IgAN patients reflects early but promising biomarker data

us.priceK.note: IgAN specialty pricing anchored by Filspari (~$60K/yr); novel mechanism commands parity

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 30% -- speculative -- Phase 1 proof-of-concept only

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 3% of 150K; EMA pathway at phase1

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 22% -- EU access TBD; EMA pathway unclear at stage

eu.priceK.note: EU net $30K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 1% of 1500K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at phase1

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 8% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $12K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: Differentiated mechanism: fat mass reduction + lean mass gain + bone density improvement. Prior data: >6% fat reduction, up to 4% lean mass gain. ~150 participants, 24-week blinded period. Topline H2 2026. >700 subjects treated safely across 5 prior studies.

us.patientsK.note: CDC NHANES 2023: 42% US adults BMI>=30 = ~110M; ~40M eligible for anti-obesity medications (AOMs) post-insurance criteria.

eu.patientsK.note: WHO Europe 2023: ~150M obese in EU+UK; ~30M eligible for AOMs given stricter HTA BMI/comorbidity gates.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~450M obese; ~50M eligible (China, Japan, AU have growing GLP-1 reimbursement).

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Medicare excludes weight-loss indication (Part D statute); commercial coverage expanding post-SELECT CV outcomes.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- NICE/G-BA limited to BMI>=35 + comorbidities; most purchase out-of-pocket.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- Japan PMDA approved, Medicare-equivalent coverage; emerging markets mostly self-pay.

us.priceK.note: $15K/yr avg across Mounjaro ($13K), Zepbound ($12K), Wegovy ($16K), Saxenda ($15K); oral GLP-1 pending.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$8K; post-G-BA negotiation Zepbound/Wegovy in UK, Germany ~50% of US list.

row.priceK.note: $3K blended; Japan PMDA pricing ~$9K, emerging markets ~$1-2K via rebates/tiered pricing.

penPct.note: ~5% peak reflects capacity-constrained launch curve; supply expansion through 2028 unlocks more.

us.reachPct.note: Ph2 in massive 40M-patient US obesity market; 0.3% reach reflects differentiated but unproven muscle-sparing vs dominant GLP-1s

us.priceK.note: Obesity class pricing ~$12K/yr (GLP-1 net benchmark); taldefgrobep positioned similarly

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 25% -- early forecast; awaits Phase 3 confirmatory data

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 0.15% of 30000K; EMA pathway at phase2

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 18% -- EU access TBD; EMA pathway unclear at stage

eu.priceK.note: EU net $6K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 0.05% of 50000K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at phase2

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 5% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $2K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: Novel Trop2 ADC with differentiated safety profile. Confirmed responses in Phase 1 as monotherapy and in combo with Regeneron's cemiplimab (anti-PD-1). Early stage — upside optionality.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~155K new CRC/yr US; ~55K metastatic/advanced on systemic tx. Source: NCI SEER 2024, ACS.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~215K new CRC/yr; ~70K on systemic per EUROCARE-6.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~320K on-tx; China NCC reports ~560K new CRC/yr (highest globally).

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- FOLFOX/FOLFIRI + bev/cetux/pani SOC; Krazati/Lumakras KRAS G12C; Keytruda MSI-H 1L; Medicare + commercial broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE + G-BA approved all tgt rx; France ASMR III-IV; bevacizumab biosim widely used.

row.wtpPct.note: ~16% -- Japan J-DPC full coverage; China NRDL cetux + bev; EM chemo backbone.

us.priceK.note: Blended $95K: bev/cetux+chemo $125K (50% pts) + KRAS G12Ci $195K (5%) + pembro MSI-H $180K (5%) + chemo alone $30K (40%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$55K blended; EU net ~58% US; bev biosim ~50% off list.

penPct.note: ~50% peak -- tgt rx tied to biomarkers (KRAS G12C 4%, MSI-H 5%, BRAF V600E 8%, HER2 3%); broad chemo base.

us.reachPct.note: Ph1 Trop2 ADC in crowded space (Trodelvy, Dato-DXd); 2% reach reflects very early single-agent data

us.priceK.note: ADC oncology class pricing ~$180K/yr US (Trodelvy/Enhertu benchmarks)

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 20% -- speculative -- Phase 1 proof-of-concept only

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 1% of 70K; EMA pathway at phase1

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 15% -- EU access TBD; EMA pathway unclear at stage

eu.priceK.note: EU net $100K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 0.5% of 320K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at phase1

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 5% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $40K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

mega_bear.narrative: RISE epilepsy fails + degrader platform unvalidated + massive dilution
events: ✗ RISE Phase 2 epilepsy misses — no efficacy signal
✗ Graves' and IgAN degrader programs show off-target toxicity
✗ Taldefgrobep obesity data underwhelms vs GLP-1s
→ Serial dilution at declining prices, sub-$5 stock

bear.narrative: Epilepsy misses + degrader platform unproven + cash crisis
events: ✗ RISE 2/3 epilepsy misses primary endpoint
✗ BHV-1300/1400 pivotal trials delayed or underpowered
✗ Taldefgrobep obesity data underwhelming vs GLP-1s
→ Dilutive raise at distressed levels, pipeline value collapses

base.narrative: Epilepsy succeeds + one degrader progresses + obesity unclear
events: ✓ RISE 2/3 hits primary endpoint — opakalim differentiated on safety
~ BHV-1300 Graves' pivotal initiated, early data encouraging
~ BHV-1400 IgAN pivotal shows biomarker responses
~ Taldefgrobep obesity shows signal but unclear vs GLP-1 standard

bull.narrative: Epilepsy blockbuster + degrader platform validated + obesity kicker
events: ✓ Opakalim best-in-class epilepsy — paradigm shift on safety
✓ MoDE/TRAP platform validated across Graves' + IgAN
✓ Taldefgrobep carves niche in obesity (muscle-sparing differentiation)
✓ Partnership/M&A interest on degrader platform

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Multi-franchise biotech — epilepsy + Graves' + obesity + degrader platform
events: ✓ RISE epilepsy blockbuster — first novel mechanism in 15 years
✓ Graves' degrader first-in-class — replaces antithyroid drugs
✓ Taldefgrobep adds lean mass to GLP-1 weight loss — combo blockbuster
✓ Platform valued at $5B+ standalone — J&J/AbbVie acquisition target

lastData.title: Opakalim OLE epilepsy data (JPM)
points: Majority of 6-month OLE patients: ≥50% seizure reduction
Exceptional tolerability — low CNS adverse events
BHV-1300 first Graves' patient: complete TSI suppression
BHV-1400 first IgAN patients: biomarker + clinical responses

nextCatalyst.title: RISE 2/3 opakalim focal epilepsy pivotal topline
thresholds: Primary: % reduction in seizure frequency vs placebo
Key differentiator: CNS safety profile vs existing ASMs
Also H2 2026: taldefgrobep obesity Phase 2 topline

risks: Cash runway ~12 months — burn ~$160M/quarter, will need to raise
No revenue — pure pipeline play, all assets pre-pivotal
Troriluzole CRL + MDD miss eroded credibility
Epilepsy RISE 2/3 is binary survival event
Degrader platform unproven in pivotal trials
Obesity: taldefgrobep faces GLP-1 juggernaut comparison
High short interest (11.6% of float) — squeeze or collapse
Dilution risk very high (30% dilution in bear case)

catalysts.title: BHV-1400 IgA nephropathy pivotal trial initiation
catalysts._rationale: Company completed 4Q25 FDA meeting to align on pivotal study design for early 2026 initiation

catalysts.title: BHV-1300 Graves' disease pivotal trial initiation
catalysts._rationale: Pivotal trial initiation planned for 2026 based on positive early patient data

catalysts.title: BHV-7000 focal epilepsy Phase 3 readout
catalysts._rationale: RISE focal epilepsy topline results expected in H1 2026 per company guidance.
